refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. The refinement clarifies the requirement by specifying the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring the FR remains solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability
Implement continuous monitoring and predictive maintenance to minimize downtime
Incorporate modular design to allow quick replacement or repair of faulty parts
Establish operational procedures and safety plans that support high availability
Use fault detection and isolation mechanisms to quickly identify and address failures
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy may require coordination between components
Failover mechanisms must be well integrated with monitoring systems
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy as a DP supports independence by isolating failures and enabling failover without tightly coupling components. However, integration with monitoring and operational procedures is necessary but can be managed at the IM level.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy alone may not achieve target availability if monitoring and maintenance are insufficient
Operational policies must align with design to realize availability targets
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance as separate DPs or as part of the implementation modules to ensure comprehensive coverage of availability factors.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing system safety, including failover and redundancy of critical components

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or safety events occur

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status and sends safety-related telemetry data

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many functions beyond safety, leading to high diffusion of failover logic.
Contains physical components but failover logic may span multiple subsystems, causing moderate diffusion.
Focuses on remote control; failover of critical components is peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Specialized in safety and failover; high cohesion and low diffusion for the DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The failover capability is best contained within the Safety Services module, which is specialized for managing system safety and redundancy, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for physical component status may cause minor diffusion.
If failover requires coordination with Teleoperation or NGHE Solution, interfaces must be well defined to avoid coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of designing the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target. Redundancy and failover are standard and effective design parameters to meet high availability requirements, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for physical component status may cause minor diffusion.
If failover requires coordination with Teleoperation or NGHE Solution modules, interfaces must be well defined to avoid coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2) for the failover DP, indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. Other modules have higher diffusion scores, suggesting that centralizing failover logic in Safety Services is appropriate to minimize diffusion and improve maintainability.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible as Safety Services module provides clear interfaces (SafetyAlert and Telemetry) to monitor and manage failover events, enabling measurement of operational availability against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the failover DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well justified, with explicit ownership and defined interfaces, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Define and document clear interface contracts between Safety Services and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules to manage dependencies and minimize coupling.
Establish monitoring and reporting mechanisms within Safety Services to provide evidence for operational availability verification.
Consider periodic review of module containment and diffusion scores as system evolves to ensure failover logic remains well contained.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current level of decomposition is sound and well-justified. The DP of designing with redundant critical components effectively meets the FR, and the IM allocation to the Safety Services module is clear with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition of the FR into more detailed sub-FRs is prudent to ensure comprehensive coverage and traceability of all aspects of operational availability and failover mechanisms.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure redundancy of critical components to achieve failover capability
Monitor and detect component failures promptly
Switch operations seamlessly to backup components upon failure
Maintain communication and coordination with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules
Provide real-time operational availability metrics and alerts

revise.why: Define and document clear interface contracts between Safety Services and dependent modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, NGHE Solution) to manage dependencies and minimize coupling, as recommended in the gate results.

assumptions: Redundancy and failover are effective and accepted design parameters for high availability.
Safety Services module has sufficient containment and low diffusion to centralize failover logic.
Interfaces between modules can be clearly defined and managed.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for physical component status causing minor diffusion.
Coordination with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules may introduce coupling risks if interfaces are not well defined.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing operational availability meets or exceeds 0.97.
Documentation of interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms within Safety Services module.
Periodic reviews of module containment and diffusion scores as system evolves.
